subject: [RFC PATCH v2 2/9] memcg: charge kmem pages and slab objects against per-node objcg

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch updates the way memory cgroup (memcg) charges kmem pages and slab objects against per-node object cgroups (objcgs). It introduces a new helper function __current_obj_cgroup() that takes a node ID as an argument, allowing for more accurate charging of memory against the correct node's objcg. The patch also updates callers in the kmem accounting path to use this new helper function, ensuring that per-node charge attribution and vmstat batching are correctly routed to the intended sibling of the same memcg. This change addresses a problem where allocations on one node were being charged against an objcg whose nid did not match the allocation's physical node, leading to incorrect charging and attribution.
